section_b-duties_responsibility_1: 1.	Animal Husbandry and Daily Care •	Provide routine feeding, watering, cleaning, and bedding of mouse cages. •	Monitor general health, behavior, and welfare of mice, reporting any abnormalities promptly. •	Maintain hygiene and sanitation of the mouse facility to prevent disease and contamination.
section_b-duties_responsibility_2: 2.	Breeding, Colony Management and Support for Preclinical Studies •	Establish and maintain mouse breeding colonies for preclinical antivenom studies.. •	Wean, sex, and appropriately allocate mice to experimental studies or for snake feeding. •	Keep accurate records of breeding, weaning, and colony inventory. •	Assist researchers with handling and preparation of mice for preclinical antivenom efficacy experiments. •	Ensure proper grouping, labelling, and allocation of mice for experimental protocols.
section_b-duties_responsibility_3: 3.	Provision of Mice as Feed for Venomous Snakes •	Prepare and supply mice as feed for snakes  •	Coordinate with herpetarium staff to meet feeding schedules and requirements.
section_b-duties_responsibility_4: 4.	Record-Keeping and Compliance •	Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all husbandry, breeding, and allocation activities. •	Ensure compliance with institutional animal care and use guidelines, biosafety, and ethical approvals.
section_b-duties_responsibility_5: 5.	Training and Professional Development •	Participate in training on animal care, welfare, biosafety, and relevant laboratory techniques. •	Stay updated on best practices for rodent care in research settings.
section_d-improve_skill: technical_professional_skills ict_skills communication_skills leadership_management policy_analysis_and_implementation financial_management monitoring_and_evaluation
section_f-relevant_training_curr_role_1: Laboratory Animal Husbandry and Welfare Advanced training in proper care, handling, and welfare of laboratory mice, including environmental enrichment and stress minimization.
section_f-relevant_training_curr_role_2: Rodent Breeding and Colony Management Skills in breeding strategies, genetic management, weaning, sexing, and maintaining healthy, productive colonies.
section_f-relevant_training_curr_role_3: Animal Handling and Restraint Techniques Safe and humane handling methods to support both routine care and experimental procedures.
section_f-relevant_training_curr_role_4: Preclinical Research Support Techniques Basic training in procedures used in antivenom efficacy studies (e.g., dosing preparation, observation, and data recording).
section_f-relevant_training_curr_role_5: Biosafety and Biosecurity Practices Proper use of PPE, contamination control, waste disposal, and infection prevention in animal facilities.
section_f-relevant_training_curr_role_6: Ethical and Regulatory Compliance Understanding institutional guidelines, animal ethics (e.g., humane endpoints), and compliance standards for animal research.
section_f-career_progression_training_1: Advanced Laboratory Animal Science Training To deepen my understanding of animal models, welfare standards, and best practices in research animal care.
section_f-career_progression_training_2: Certification in Animal Care and Use (Ethics & Compliance) Training on ethical frameworks and regulatory requirements (e.g., animal care and use protocols) to qualify for higher responsibilities in research oversight.
section_f-career_progression_training_3: Preclinical Research Techniques Hands-on training in experimental procedures such as dosing, sample collection, monitoring endpoints, and data recording to actively support antivenom studies.
section_f-career_progression_training_4: Biosafety and Laboratory Safety Certification Advanced knowledge in biosafety levels, risk assessment, and safe handling of biological materials.
section_f-career_progression_training_5: Supervisory and Leadership Skills Training in team coordination, communication, and facility management to prepare for senior or supervisory roles.
section_f-career_progression_training_6: Continuing Professional Development (Workshops & Short Courses) Participation in seminars and workshops related to laboratory animal science, toxicology, and preclinical research.

section_f-career_progression_training_1: Digital & Modern Lab Skills e.g. Laboratory Information Systems, basic data analysis (Excel) Record keeping & reporting
section_f-career_progression_training_2: Advanced Laboratory Skills Training e.g. good Laboratory Practice, Quality Management Systems, Laboratory Auditing & Quality Control Equipment Calibration & Maintenance
section_f-career_progression_training_3: Computer & Lab Systems Training e.g. basic computer skills (Excel, data entry) Laboratory Information Systems
section_f-career_progression_training_4: Equipment & Quality Training e.g. use & maintenance of:  Centrifuges Microscopes Lab analyzers
section_f-career_progression_training_5: Technical Skills Training e.g. Microscopy techniques (slide preparation, staining) Microbiology basics (culturing, identifying organisms) Hematology basics (blood grouping, CBC basics) Clinical chemistry procedure
section_f-career_progression_training_6: Essential On-the-Job Trainings e.g. good Laboratory Practice (GLP) Laboratory Safety & Biosafety Sample collection & handling, Infection Prevention & Control (IPC)
section_i-new_skill_you_need_1: Digital laboratory skills, I will need skills in using laboratory information systems, computer data entry, and digital reporting as laboratories become more computerized.
section_i-new_skill_you_need_2: Operation of automated laboratory equipment, I will need the ability to operate, calibrate, and maintain modern automated analyzers and machines used in laboratory testing.
section_i-new_skill_you_need_3: Data analysis and interpretation, I will need basic skills in analyzing laboratory results using tools like Excel and understanding data for accurate reporting.
section_i-new_skill_you_need_4: Quality control and quality assurance, I will need knowledge of quality management systems, including standards like ISO, to ensure accuracy and reliability of laboratory results.
section_i-new_skill_you_need_5: Advanced laboratory techniques, I will need to improve my technical skills in areas such as microbiology, hematology, and sample handling to grow into a higher role.
section_i-new_skill_you_need_6: Biosafety and infection prevention, I will need updated knowledge in biosafety, waste management, and infection control to handle samples safely and prevent contamination.
section_j-additional_comment: I suggest that training programs should be made more accessible and practical for laboratory assistants by including hands-on sessions and on-the-job training. There should be regular refresher courses to keep staff updated with new technologies, especially in automation and digital laboratory systems. Employers should support staff by providing time, funding, and equal opportunities to attend training. Additionally, more online and flexible learning options should be introduced to accommodate busy work schedules. Mentorship programs and career guidance would also help laboratory assistants understand clear pathways for growth and professional development.
